skill.name: full-stack-orchestration-full-stack-feature
skill.description: Orchestrates full-stack feature development across backend, frontend, and infrastructure layers using AI subagents
skill.summary: API-first full-stack development workflow coordinating specialized AI agents from architecture through deployment
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 25 patterns but all are false positives - SKILL.md is documentation describing workflow prompts, not executable code. The skill orchestrates AI subagents for full-stack development. External commands risk factor present in documentation references only. No actual shell execution, cryptographic operations, or network calls in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation References External Tools
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documentation references external tools and commands (Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, etc.) but these are conceptual workflow steps executed by AI subagents, not direct shell execution by this skill
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation describes workflow steps using Task tool subagents - actual execution delegated to specialized AI agents with their own security contexts

content.user_title: Orchestrate Full-Stack Features Across All Layers
content.value_statement: Building full-stack features requires coordinating multiple specialists and technologies. This skill manages the entire workflow from database design through deployment using AI subagents for each layer.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates database architecture design with specialized AI subagents
Manages parallel backend and frontend implementation workflows
Orchestrates API contract testing and end-to-end test automation
Handles infrastructure setup including Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines
Implements observability stack with distributed tracing and monitoring
Performs performance optimization across database, backend, and frontend layers
limitations: Requires access to Task tool with subagent support for execution
Does not directly execute code - delegates all implementation to AI subagents
Technology stack must be specified via configuration options
Each phase depends on outputs from previous phases creating sequential dependencies

use_cases.title: Enterprise Feature Development
use_cases.description: Launch complex features requiring database changes, new API endpoints, frontend components, and infrastructure updates with full testing and deployment automation
use_cases.target_user: Senior full-stack developers at product companies

use_cases.title: Startup MVP Delivery
use_cases.description: Rapidly build and deploy minimum viable products by orchestrating AI subagents to handle all development layers from schema design to production deployment
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and small engineering teams

use_cases.title: Legacy System Modernization
use_cases.description: Systematically migrate monolithic applications to microservices architecture with proper API contracts, containerization, and observability

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a user profile feature with avatar upload, bio editing, and privacy settings. Stack: Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Deploy to AWS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick feature addition for existing application

prompt_templates.title: API-First Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a payment processing integration with Stripe. Start with OpenAPI spec, then implement backend webhooks, frontend checkout flow, and comprehensive testing. Include HIPAA compliance considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Full Product Launch
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete SaaS analytics dashboard from scratch. Include user authentication, data ingestion pipeline, real-time charts, subscription billing, and multi-tenant architecture. Deploy to GCP with blue-green deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Microservices Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Migrate our monolithic e-commerce checkout to microservices. Create separate services for cart, payments, inventory, and shipping. Implement event-driven architecture with message queues, distributed tracing, and circuit breakers. Maintain zero-downtime during migration.

output_examples.input: Add two-factor authentication to user login
output: Phase 1 Complete: Database schema for TOTP secrets, backend service design with authentication flow, frontend component architecture for setup wizard
Phase 2 Complete: Implemented TOTP generation endpoints, QR code display component, backup codes management, SMS fallback integration
Phase 3 Complete: Contract tests validating API responses, E2E tests for login flows, security audit confirming OWASP compliance
Phase 4 Complete: Docker containers deployed to Kubernetes, feature flags configured for gradual rollout, monitoring dashboards active
Database: PostgreSQL listen/notify setup with notification queue tables and user preference schemas
Backend: WebSocket gateway with Redis pub/sub, notification templates, delivery tracking
Frontend: React notification center component with real-time updates, preference management modal
Infrastructure: Horizontal pod autoscaling configured, message persistence enabled, delivery metrics dashboard

best_practices: Define clear API contracts before implementation begins to enable parallel development
Use correlation IDs across all services for distributed tracing and debugging
Implement feature flags for all new functionality to enable safe progressive rollouts
anti_patterns: Skipping contract testing leads to integration failures between frontend and backend
Deploying without observability makes production incidents impossible to diagnose
Hardcoding environment-specific values instead of using configuration management

faq.question: What technology stacks are supported?
faq.answer: The workflow supports any modern stack. Specify via the stack parameter, for example React/FastAPI/PostgreSQL or Next.js/Django/MongoDB. Subagents specialize in Python, Node.js, Golang, and frontend frameworks.

faq.question: Can I run phases in parallel?
faq.answer: Phase 1 must complete sequentially as each step builds on previous outputs. Phase 2 implementation tasks can run in parallel. Phase 3 and 4 require Phase 2 completion.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code directly?
faq.answer: No. This skill orchestrates work by delegating to specialized AI subagents via the Task tool. Each subagent handles implementation for its domain expertise.

faq.question: How are secrets and credentials handled?
faq.answer: Credentials should be configured in your deployment platform environment variables. The skill documentation references secrets management but does not handle credentials directly.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The compliance parameter supports GDPR, HIPAA, and SOC2. Each triggers specific security controls, audit logging, and documentation requirements in the workflow.

faq.question: Can I customize the testing depth?
faq.answer: Yes. Set testing_depth to essential for rapid prototyping or comprehensive for production releases. Essential includes unit tests only. Comprehensive adds contract, integration, E2E, and load tests.
